loci.rationale: Arabidopsis canonical reference; covers BAR/TAIR/ATTED Arabidopsis-only tools + KEGG ath: native path + Europe PMC; densest annotation coverage. KEGG happens to be unannotated for this specific locus → expects_exception NotFoundError (same shape as v1.5 barley/poplar/brachypodium).
Rice canonical (v0.7 / v1.4.0 live test locus); KEGG bridge (osa), Phytozome, Gramene, Ensembl /xrefs

v1.7 seed 3: Phytozome happy-path via the genome's NATIVE gene id. The Ensembl-style Os01g0100100 raises NotFoundError (kept as a namespace-mismatch guard) — not data drift. Native id + organism_name echo confirmed live via scripts/probe_phytozome_namespace.json.
Maize v1.4.0 live test locus; KEGG bridge (zma), Phytozome. MaizeGDB rotation handled by tolerance.
Soybean v1.4.0 live test locus; KEGG bridge (gmx, Glyma.→GLYMA_ normalizer fires), Phytozome

v1.7 seed 3: Phytozome happy-path via the genome's NATIVE gene id. The Ensembl-style GLYMA_01G001700 raises NotFoundError (kept as a namespace-mismatch guard) — not data drift. Native id + organism_name echo confirmed live via scripts/probe_phytozome_namespace.json.
Barley v1.5 ship-locus. Bridge fires, KEGG returns 0 pathways → NotFoundError raised.
Poplar v1.5 ship-locus. Same expected-exception shape as barley.
Brachypodium v1.5 ship-locus. Same expected-exception shape as barley.
Wheat v1.5 falsified-organism: KEGG raises OrganismNotSupported pre-HTTP; positive for Ensembl/Phytozome
Tomato v1.5 re-falsified: KEGG raises OrganismNotSupported; positive for Ensembl/Phytozome/Europe PMC

KEGG happy-path coverage (v1.7) — ath:AT1G01050 carries 1 KEGG pathway membership(s); validates the bridge SUCCESS path (vs the chr1-first-gene 0-pathway expects_exception entry for this organism). Discovered via scripts/probe_kegg_happy_path.py.

loci.rationale: v1.8 Phytozome happy-path: native gene id round-trip-confirmed live (scripts/probe_phytozome_namespace.json). Broadens Phytozome coverage to all 12 supported organisms; organism_name asserted via startswith prefix so an assembly-version bump does not FAIL.
